name: Wardell Gray
abstract: Wardell Gray (February 13, 1921 – May 25, 1955) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: Before I did anything I played with Wardell Gray, that was one of my first jobs, so I worked all over town with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: We had Wardell Gray, Clark Terry, Gus Johnson on drums, Freddie Green on guitar, me on bass...
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Lewis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: The front line was Wardell Gray, Willie Smith, Roy Eldridge, and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: I was gigging with Dexter and Wardell; they had a group, two tenors and a trumpet... Wardell got a chance to make a recording for Prestige Records, and he asked me to do it with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: later on, when he left, they decided to use two altos and a tenor; then Wardell Gray came in playing the other alto saxophone.

about_quotes.speaker: Elvin Jones
about_quotes.evidence: So your first professional gig was playing with Wardell Gray? Yeah, (laughs) with Wardell Gray!
about_quotes.doc_title: Elvin Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
